content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File

Date : Dec. 8, 1976

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Ken Finney

Subject: Telephone conversation with Jay Greenwood of December 7, 1976.

In a telephone conversation with Jay Greenwood, he and I decided that it would be permissible for a candidate filing a campaign statement in January which would be applicable to transactions occurring during 1976 to file a consolidated campaign statement as authorized by amendments to the Act which become effective January 1, 1977. We concluded that the candidate must disclose all information required pursuant to the law as it was during 1976, but we thought it appropriate to permit the candidate to file a consolidated statement since this would be easier and it would not result in any lack of disclosure. It would merely mean that instead of a statement from the candidate's committee and from the candidate there will only be one statement.

KF:jo

[The image appears to be extremely faded or low contrast, making the text largely illegible. While there seem to be some words and possibly paragraph structures present in the upper portion of the page, I cannot reliably transcribe the specific content due to the poor visibility of the text.]
